title: helleranthus quadrangulatus
intro: Helleranthus quadrangulatus, also known as the blue vervain or swamp vervain, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its blue flowers and four-angled stems. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Helleranthus quadrangulatus include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, promote sleep, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, in addition to its antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
